summary: preinstall hook fetches and evals remote code from attacker-controlled Vercel endpoint
details: package.json declares a preinstall hook that runs index.d.js on npm install. index.d.js decodes a base64 blob into an async IIFE that fetches a script from https://everydaynodechecer-39143n.vercel.app/api/key?mem=root1 and executes the response body via eval. The eval identifier is reconstructed from the character-code array [101,118,97,108] and looked up on globalThis to evade string-based scanners, and the fetch URL is hidden inside the base64 payload. Both the executed function name and the network destination are obfuscated in a lifecycle script, giving whoever controls the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at npm install time. The package name also resembles the legitimate markdown-table package, consistent with typosquat delivery.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
